page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores with a 2.0 GHz clock speed, operating on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is equipped with 24.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 500 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present.

Benchmark results place this server's CPU capabilities in the average range, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2219.48 and a multi-core score of 27275.02. PassMark CPU testing yields a score of 17153.85. However, memory performance shows some limitations; the PassMark memory score is categorized as weak at 1623.4, and single-threaded compression tasks perform poorly, with gzip compression at 18.92 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 6.48 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches an average tier of 41.52 GB/sec.

In terms of workload execution, the server handles static web serving at an average throughput of 7.88 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 11.99 million operations per second. For machine learning, small model (135M) LLM inference runs at average speeds, though prompt processing for medium (7B) models drops to a weak 16.57 tokens/sec. Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded processing, web serving, and database operations that do not require dedicated CPU cores or high memory density.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 24.0 GB of RAM, and a 500 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor delivers average multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 27275.02 and a PassMark CPU score of 17153.85. However, memory performance is a notable tradeoff, with a weak PassMark memory score of 1623.4 and poor single-threaded compression benchmarks. The server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations, achieving 11.99 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 7.88 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. It offers a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high core counts without dedicated CPU guarantees.

meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is an x86_64 server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe SSD, suited for multi-threaded web serving.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC virtual server offering twelve vCPUs and local NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
24.0 GB of system memory paired with a 500 GB local NVMe SSD
Average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 27275.02
Tradeoffs include weak memory performance and poor single-threaded compression
Suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and multi-threaded processing
